Market Intelligence Brief
Generated 11:32 ET
Extreme P/C divergence signals dispersion trades as mega-cap earnings loom
KEY TAKEAWAYS
  • 1. Index puts heavy (1.40 P/C) while single-names tilt call-heavy (0.63 P/C) — institutional dispersion hedge ahead of earnings.
  • 2. Implied correlation crashes to 0.05 with positive dealer gamma creating ideal dispersion environment.
  • 3. Mega-cap earnings in 5 days with FOMC follow-through creates binary vol repricing risk.
NARRATIVE — greed with dispersion hedging

The most striking signal today is the extreme P/C divergence — index puts running 1.40x while single-names favor calls at 0.63x — suggesting institutions are hedging portfolio exposure while targeting individual earnings winners. This divergence intensifies with implied correlation collapsing to 0.05, the lowest stock-specific regime reading, creating textbook conditions for long single-name vol, short index vol dispersion trades. Options flow confirms this institutional positioning with massive SPY put blocks (257k contracts in 710P) offsetting concentrated call demand in earnings names. Notable is the $+2.7B positive dealer gamma in both SPY and QQQ, which dampens spot moves and reduces hedge decay — favorable for maintaining dispersion positions into the earnings gauntlet. The VIX term structure sits in healthy +2.3pt contango, pricing subdued near-term risk despite next week's GDP/FOMC double catalyst. ARM's backwardation stands out given the CPU market entry news driving +14.8% spot performance, yet options are pricing elevated near-term risk despite bullish technicals. This disconnect, combined with 188% rich IV in META ahead of earnings, suggests vol sellers are demanding premium for binary event risk. The concentration of mega-cap earnings (MSFT, META, GOOG, AMZN) within 5 days creates unusual calendar pressure where single-name realized vol could easily exceed index vol.

Layer 1: Sentiment +53Layer 2: GEX +$2.7BLayer 3: P/C divergence 1.40 vs 0.63Layer 4: IC 0.05 stock-specific
ARMVOL PLAY
CPU entry backwardation
ARM surges 14.8% on CPU market entry news yet options price elevated near-term risk in backwardation — unusual disconnect between bullish spot momentum and cautious vol structure. Technical score 85.5 despite death cross signals momentum override.

Earnings vol premium
META IV trading 188% rich vs sector ETF ahead of Apr 29 earnings, the highest premium among mega-caps. Druckenmiller's full Q4 exit contrasts with concentrated institutional call demand, suggesting event vol sellers demanding maximum premium.

Financial sector hedge
Massive 10k XLF Jun 48P block at $0.42 represents $4.2M premium commitment to financial sector downside protection. Positioned through FOMC and potential rate cut cycle, suggesting institutional concern over NIM compression despite current sector strength.
